title: polycarpon tetraphyllum subsp. alsinifolium
intro: Polycarpon tetraphyllum subsp. alsinifolium, also known as Alsine tetraphylla or Four-Leafed Alsine, is a subspecies of the Polycarpon tetraphyllum plant, a flowering plant in the Caryophyllaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European folk medicine, where it is valued for its diuretic and antiseptic properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of urinary tract issues, reduction of inflammation, and improvement of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Polycarpon tetraphyllum subsp. alsinifolium can be found in tea and infusion forms, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
